environment.subagent_permissions: Background subagents cannot answer permission prompts. Before spawning runs, allowlist in .claude/settings.local.json: tctl, tsh, aws (read-only subcommands), and the fixture bin/ wrapper paths, or the agents fall back to workarounds (proxy ping endpoint, reading stub source) that degrade fidelity.
environment.working_directory: Run evals outside the teleport monorepo. Inside it, baseline agents find the discovery module source at integrations/terraform-modules/teleport/discovery/aws and the docs pages, an advantage they do not have in real use.
environment.grading: The grader, not the agent, runs terraform init -backend=false and terraform validate per generated config. Check for agent-created .terraform/.tfstate before the grader inits.
environment.model: Spawn the with-skill and baseline run agents with model: sonnet. Keep graders on the session model so grading quality stays constant while only the system under test changes.

evals.prompt: We use Teleport Cloud, our dev tenant is wispy-wood.beams.sh. I want our EC2 instances in us-west-2 tagged env=dev to enroll automatically. Write the discovery Terraform for me in a new directory here. I've already reviewed the approach, so treat the plan as approved, but do NOT run terraform init or apply — I'll apply it myself.
evals.expected_output: versions.tf and main.tf: teleport provider pinned to the cluster major, proxy wispy-wood.beams.sh:443, discovery_group cloud-discovery-group, one EC2 matcher, four named outputs, no terraform executed.
assertions: Terraform files written: a new directory in outputs/ contains .tf configuration (not just prose)
terraform validate passes (grader-run)
Module source is terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/aws
teleport_proxy_public_addr is wispy-wood.beams.sh:443 with no https:// scheme
teleport_discovery_group_name is cloud-discovery-group
Exactly one matcher: types ["ec2"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["dev"]}
Teleport provider version constraint pins major 18 with an upper bound below 19.0.0 (e.g. ~> 18.0); a bare >= floor fails
create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider is NOT set to false (account 278576220453 has no OIDC provider for wispy-wood.beams.sh)
Outputs defined with exactly these names: integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, discovery_role_arn; the same values under different names fail
No apply or init executed by the agent: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file

evals.prompt: Set up AWS auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh: EC2 instances in every region tagged team=core, and EKS clusters in us-west-2 tagged env=staging. Also turn on app discovery for the EKS clusters. The plan is approved — write the Terraform into a new directory but don't apply anything.
evals.expected_output: Two matchers (EC2 all-regions team=core; EKS us-west-2 env=staging with kube_app_discovery), four named outputs, no terraform executed.
assertions: Terraform files written: a new directory in outputs/ contains .tf configuration
terraform validate passes (grader-run)
Module source is terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/aws
EC2 matcher present: types ["ec2"], regions ["*"], tags {team: ["core"]}
EKS matcher present: types ["eks"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["staging"]}, kube_app_discovery = true
kube_app_discovery is NOT enabled on the EC2 matcher
teleport_proxy_public_addr is wispy-wood.beams.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is cloud-discovery-group
Teleport provider version constraint pins major 18 with an upper bound below 19.0.0 (e.g. ~> 18.0); a bare >= floor fails
Outputs defined with exactly these names: integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, discovery_role_arn; the same values under different names fail
No apply or init executed by the agent: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file

evals.prompt: I have an existing Terraform project in ./infra. Add EKS auto-discovery to it for clusters tagged env=dev in us-west-2 on our Teleport Cloud tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh. Plan approved, but do not run terraform — I'll review and apply later.
evals.expected_output: Module block added inside infra/ labeled aws_discovery, providers extended in place, fixture resources untouched, no terraform executed.
assertions: The discovery module block is added inside the existing infra/ project; creating a separate new directory fails
The module block is labeled aws_discovery (skill convention; a different label fails this assertion only)

required_providers extended in place with teleport, http, and tls entries, preserving the pre-existing aws entry (skill convention; terraform validate passes without http/tls, so this discriminates convention, not validity)
EKS matcher: types ["eks"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["dev"]}
teleport_proxy_public_addr is wispy-wood.beams.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is cloud-discovery-group
Pre-existing resources untouched: aws_s3_bucket.artifacts and aws_s3_bucket_versioning.artifacts identical to the fixture
terraform validate passes in infra/ (grader-run)
No apply or init executed by the agent: infra/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file

evals.prompt: Discover our EC2 instances tagged role=web in us-east-1 and enroll them in our self-hosted Teleport cluster teleport.corp.example.com.
evals.expected_output: Agent confirms no discovery service is running, tells the user one must be deployed first, and stops without producing configuration.
assertions: Agent checked for a running discovery service via the stubbed tctl inventory list --services=discovery (which returns nothing)

Agent stopped: outputs/ contains no configuration artifacts of any kind — no .tf files, no YAML manifests, no IAM policy documents, no SSM documents, no install scripts. A run that ships an alternative enrollment kit instead of stopping fails
final-response.md tells the user a deployed discovery service is required before discovery can be configured (the skill does not set one up)
Agent did not invent a discovery_group value or proceed with placeholder configuration
Agent did not run the real tctl/tsh/aws binaries against a live cluster or account (no live resource names in the response)

evals.prompt: Enroll our EKS clusters in us-east-1 tagged env=prod into our Teleport Cloud cluster acme.teleport.sh. Plan approved — write the Terraform but don't apply it.
evals.expected_output: create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider = false because the stubbed account already has an OIDC provider for acme.teleport.sh; one EKS matcher; four named outputs; no terraform executed.
assertions: Terraform files written: outputs/ contains .tf configuration
terraform validate passes (grader-run)
create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider = false set in the module block (stubbed aws returns an existing OIDC provider whose Url is acme.teleport.sh)
teleport_proxy_public_addr is acme.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is cloud-discovery-group
EKS matcher: types ["eks"], regions ["us-east-1"], tags {env: ["prod"]}
Teleport provider version constraint pins major 18 with an upper bound below 19.0.0 (e.g. ~> 18.0); a bare >= floor fails
Outputs defined with exactly these names: integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, discovery_role_arn; the same values under different names fail
No apply or init executed by the agent: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file

evals.prompt: Set up EC2 auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud dev tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh for instances in us-west-2 tagged teleport-discovery-eval=true. Write the Terraform, apply it, and verify the resources were created. auto_approve: true
evals.expected_output: Apply exits 0 with the four outputs; integration, discovery_config (picked up by the running discovery service), OIDC provider, and provision token all verifiable live. Run serially, with-skill arm only; terraform destroy and verify removal after grading.
assertions: terraform apply exited 0 and printed all four outputs: integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, discovery_role_arn
tctl get integrations shows the aws-oidc integration named in the integration_name output
tctl get discovery_config shows the config named in the discovery_config_name output, with matcher types ["ec2"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {teleport-discovery-eval: ["true"]}
The discovery_config's discovery_group matches the group the running discovery service uses (status reaches DISCOVERY_CONFIG_STATE_SYNCING)
An IAM OIDC provider for wispy-wood.beams.sh exists in account 278576220453 (matching the oidc_provider_arn output)
A provision token for the EC2 join was created (EC2 matcher present)
Agent verified the resources itself per references/monitor-troubleshoot.md and reported the verification in final-response.md
